sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root עָלָה)-

(1) an ascent, a place by which one goes up, Neh 12:37. With suff. מַעֲלָיו its ascent (sing. compare Hebr. Gramm. § 90, 9), Eze 40:31.

(2) a lofty place-(a) a stage, a platform, Neh 9:4.-(b) an acclivity, a hill. מַעֲלֵה הָעִיר 1Sa 9:11 מַעֲלֵה הַזֵּתִים the acclivity or mount of Olives, 2Sa 15:30 מַעֲלֵה אֲדֻמִּים Jos 15:7 18:17 , on the borders of the tribes of Judah and Benjamin, מַעֲלֵה עַקְרַבִּים (“the acclivity of scorpions”), Num 34:4 Jos 15:3, on the southern borders of Palestine.
